question_title: How many barrels of crude oil will be in the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve on April 30, 2026?
summary:
The SPR was at 415.064M barrels as of week ending March 27, 2026, with barely any drawdown despite the March 11 announcement. The first tranche of 45.2M barrels was contracted March 20 via exchange contracts for delivery April 1-May 31. The resolution data point will be from the EIA report released April 29 (covering week ending ~April 24), giving about 24 active drawdown days in April.

Key calibration: In 2022, the Biden SPR release (direct sales) drew ~23M barrels in April at ~823K/day. The current exchange mechanism is more complex and slower than direct sales, and only 45.2M of 86M offered was subscribed, suggesting moderate drawdown. My base case projects ~15-17M barrel drawdown through the measurement date, yielding an SPR level around 398-400M barrels. The distribution is mildly left-skewed due to tail scenarios of accelerated drawdown (second solicitation, conversion to direct sales) vs. a thin right tail (ceasefire scenario where contracts still proceed).

factors.description: Starting SPR level of 415,064K barrels (week ending March 27, 2026) confirmed by EIA weekly data. Firm anchor point.

First tranche of 45.2M barrels contracted for delivery April 1-May 31. Pro-rata April portion (~24 days through April 24) is ~17.8M if evenly distributed, but exchange mechanism is slower than direct sales.
2022 precedent: April 2022 saw ~23M barrel drawdown (~823K/day) via direct sales. Exchange contracts are slower and more complex, suggesting lower drawdown rate.
Employ America analysis: deliveries won't begin until April, exchange prioritizes premium over speed, mechanism complexity limits pace. Only 52% of offered volume subscribed.
DOE claims 'record speeds', Bryan Mound can deliver ~700K bpd, strong political pressure to accelerate. Partially offsets exchange complexity.
Possible ceasefire/de-escalation reducing drawdown urgency, though signed contracts would still proceed.
factors.conditional: If ceasefire or major de-escalation occurs

factors.description: Possible second solicitation or conversion to direct emergency sales accelerating drawdown beyond first 45.2M tranche.
factors.conditional: If DOE issues additional emergency sales

reasoning:
**Forecast Summary: SPR Level on April 30, 2026**

- **Median estimate: ~399 million barrels**
- **80% CI: [390M, 404M]**
- **Expected drawdown from current level: ~15-17M barrels**

Key reasoning:
- The SPR stood at 415.064M barrels as of March 27 with minimal drawdown so far
- 45.2M barrels contracted via exchange for delivery April 1-May 31
- Exchange contracts are slower than direct sales (2022 precedent)
- Pro-rata ~17.8M barrels through April 24 measurement date, adjusted down for ramp-up and exchange complexity
- Distribution is left-skewed: accelerated drawdown scenarios (second solicitation, political pressure) create a wider downside tail than upside scenarios

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve as the number of barrels of crude oil in the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR), as reported by the most recent weekly [EIA Petroleum Supply report](https://www.eia.gov/petroleum/supply/weekly/) released on or before April 30, 2026.
fine_print:
If no reports are published by the EIA between April 20 and April 30, 2026, the question will be annulled.

***
This question's information (resolution criteria, fine print, background info, etc) is synced with an [original identical question](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42831) which opened on 2026-03-25 17:00:00. This question will resolve based on the resolution criteria and fine print of the linked original question. However, if this question would resolve differently than the original question, then this question will be annulled. Additionally, if the original question's resolution could have been known before this question opened, then this question will be annulled.
